title: Simple causal stint-boundary features instead of rolling pace trends
summary: Add low-risk current and previous stint-state features while avoiding rolling pace, residual pace, and same-lap peer features that repeatedly regressed.
rationale: TyreLife, race phase, compound, and degradation already carry strong signal, but raw stint context may still add information around stint boundaries. Broad rolling pace, residual pace, lag, or same-lap peer features are noisy and can regress, so only simple causal stint-state features should be tested.
implementation_hint: Extend step-65 preprocessing with only simple causal stint-boundary features from Stint, TyreLife, previous compound, and previous PitStop. Add Stint_Ordinal, Is_FirstLapOfStint, Is_SecondLapOfStint, PriorCompoundChanged using shift(1), PriorPitStop using shift(1) if PitStop exists, CompoundChangedThisStint, and optionally TyreLife_WithinStint_Ratio using only cumulative information. Avoid rolling lap-time, residual pace, and same-lap peer aggregates.
expected_effect: Small to medium potential improvement by capturing errors around stint boundaries without the regressions seen from noisy pace-trend features.
risk: Prior-row features must be computed after sorting by Race_Year, Driver, and LapNumber and must use only shift(1). Do not use future stint length or future maximums. If Stint is absent from test, the block must be skipped.
